mission.title: Install and Configure Trivy Operator (Aqua Security)
mission.description: Trivy Operator is a Kubernetes-native security toolkit that helps you identify vulnerabilities in your container images and running workloads. It is useful for ensuring compliance and security in your Kubernetes clusters. Key trade-offs include the need for proper configuration to avoid performance overhead during scans.

steps.title: Add the Aqua Security Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Aqua Security Helm repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install the Trivy Operator.

```bash

helm repo add aqua https://aquasecurity.github.io/helm-charts

```

> This is the official repository for Aqua Security Helm charts.

steps.title: Install the Trivy Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Trivy Operator using Helm, specifying the namespace and version.

```bash

helm install trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --create-namespace --version 0.23.0

```

> Change the version as needed.

steps.title: Create a TrivyConfiguration custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource that configures the Trivy Operator's behavior.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: default
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  image:
    repository: docker.io/library/nginx
    tag: latest
EOF

```

> This example configures Trivy to scan the nginx image.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: default
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  image:
    repository: docker.io/library/nginx
    tag: latest
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Trivy Operator installation
steps.description:
This step checks that the Trivy Operator pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> You should see the Trivy Operator pod running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Trivy Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Trivy Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that any configurations and data related to the operator will be lost.

```bash

helm uninstall trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system

kubectl delete namespace trivy-system

```

> This will remove the operator but not any orphaned resources.
> This will delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Trivy Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important configurations. This step will upgrade the Trivy Operator to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -n1 kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

kubectl uncordon $(kubectl get nodes -o name)

```

> Drain the pods before upgrading.
> Change the version as needed.
> Uncordon the nodes after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n trivy-system -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -n1 kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Trivy Operator pod is CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may be due to misconfiguration or insufficient permissions.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs -n trivy-system <pod-name>' and ensure the necessary RBAC permissions are set.

troubleshooting.title: No vulnerabilities found when there should be some
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Trivy Operator may not be scanning the correct images or workloads.

**Fix:**
Verify the TrivyConfiguration resource and ensure the correct images are specified.

troubleshooting.title: Helm install fails with 'chart not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Aqua Security Helm repository may not be added correctly.

**Fix:**
Re-add the Helm repo with 'helm repo add aqua https://aquasecurity.github.io/helm-charts' and run 'helm repo update'.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl apply fails with 'error validating data'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The YAML configuration may have syntax errors.

**Fix:**
Check the YAML syntax and ensure proper indentation and formatting.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for scanning additional file types and improved performance for large deployments.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Trivy Operator pod running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n trivy-system'.
codeSnippets:

apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: default
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  image:
    repository: docker.io/library/nginx
    tag: latest
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.25 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
